row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a revenue of 716.9B USD, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by 3.7B USD. The top two companies hold a significant revenue gap of 14.7% over UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) in third place.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a Net Income of $132.2B, a significant gap of at least $11.9B from second-place NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA). This substantial lead highlights Alphabet's dominant position in the technology sector.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap of $1T from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) in second place. This substantial difference in asset base underscores JPM's dominant position in the financial sector.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) maintains its position as the leader in Total Equity, boasting a substantial 415.3B USD. The top companies are closely clustered, with a mere 3.2B USD separating GOOGL from Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) in second place.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the list with $4.1T in Total Liabilities, a significant gap from the second-largest, Bank of America Corporation (BAC) with $3.1T. This gap of $1T underscores the substantial difference in financial leverage between the two banking giants.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at $239.3B. This 43% difference highlights JPM's substantial liquidity advantage.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of $360.5B USD, a significant gap of over $120B USD from second-place Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) with a Gross Profit of $240.3B USD.
row1.operating_income_ebit: Apple Inc. (AAPL) leads the pack with an Operating Income Ebit of $133.1B USD, a significant gap of $3.6B USD from NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) in second place. This substantial lead underscores AAPL's strong financial performance in the latest fiscal year.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the revenue landscape, accounting for 43.1% of total revenue, with Consumer Cyclical leading at 22.0%. Top two sectors hold nearly half of the market's revenue.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the sector distribution by Total Assets, accounting for 64.1% of the total, a significant concentration of assets in this industry. The top 4 sectors - Financial Services, Technology, Communication Services, and Consumer Cyclical - collectively represent 96.5% of the total assets.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape, accounting for 39.2% of the total with $341.0B in net income, significantly outpacing the next closest sector. This highlights the sector's disproportionate influence on overall profitability.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin significantly outpaces UNH's 2.7%, highlighting stark differences in profitability efficiency across companies, with NVDA's strong gross margins driving its exceptional net margin. This disparity underscores the varying levels of operational efficiency and pricing power among industry leaders.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out with a remarkable 193.5% Return on Equity, but its high leverage is a concern, while JPMorgan Chase (JPM) takes on significant debt with a 11.21x Debt/Equity ratio, a potential risk to its financial health.
row4_market_trends: Alphabet (GOOGL) leads the pack with a 12-month cumulative return of +67.5%, outperforming the market. Adobe Systems (ADBE) lags behind with a -43.0% return, a stark contrast to the top performer. Market volatility persists, with a wide range of returns among high-revenue companies.
